title: Article tracking system
abstract: An article tracking system including: a plurality of tags, each tag being attached to a respective article in use, a number of tag readers each tag reader in use generates and transmits a message to tags within range of the reader, wherein the one or more tags responsive to the message selectively change operating mode from a passive to active state and if the message is a broadcast message, transmit a tag message at least partially indicative of an identity of the tag to the respective reader whereon the reader generates location data at least partially indicative of a location of at least one tag using the identity of the at least one tag and provides the location data to the at least one processing system, the at least one processing device being responsive to the location data to track the location of articles.
claims:
Claims THE CLAIMS DEFINING THE INVENTION ARE AS FOLLOWS: 1) An article tracking system including: a) a plurality of tags, each tag being attached to a respective article in use and including: i) a power supply; ii) a tag transceiver that transmits or receives messages; and iii) a tag processing device; b) a number of tag readers, each tag reader including: i) at least one reader transceiver that transmits or receives messages; and, ii) a reader processing device in communication with the at least one tag reader; and, c) at least one processing system in communication with the number of tag readers, and wherein in use each tag reader: i) generates a message; and, ii) transmits the message to one or more tags within an operating range of the reader, wherein ones of the one or more tags in a passive operating mode are responsive to the message to: (1) receive the message; (2) selectively change a tag operating mode; and, (3) if the message is a broadcast message, transmit a tag message at least partially indicative of an identity of the tag; iii) receives any tag messages; iv) generates location data at least partially indicative of a location of at least one tag using the identity of the at least one tag from any received tag messages; and, v) provides the location data to the at least one processing system, the at least one processing device being responsive to the location data to track the location of articles. 2) An article tracking system according to claim 1, wherein in the passive operating mode the tag processing device does not transmit messages. 3) An article tracking system according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein in response to a broadcast message received from the tag reader, the tag processing device: a) enters an active operating mode; and, b) transmits the tag message. 

 4) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 3, wherein the tag processing device: a) receives a sleep message from the reader; and, b) enters a sleep operating mode for a defined time period in response to receiving the sleep message, wherein in the sleep operating mode the tag processing device at least one of: i) does not respond to messages; and, ii) does not receive messages. 5) An article tracking system according to claim 4, wherein the sleep message is generated by the reader in response to receipt of the tag message. 6) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 5, wherein in response to a power message, the tag processing device enters a power off operating mode by deactivating the power supply. 7) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 6, wherein the tag message includes: a) a tag identifier indicative of the tag identity; and, b) at least one of: i) a power supply indicator indicative of a power supply status; and, ii) a temperature indicator indicative of the maximum and minimum temperature to which the tag has been exposed between interactions with a reader. 8) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 7, wherein the tag transceiver is a Bluetooth transceiver. 9) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 8, wherein the tag is labelled with information including at least one of: a) machine readable coded data indicative of a tag identifier; b) coded data indicative of the identity of the tag; and, c) visible markings indicative of an identity of the tag. 10) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 9, wherein the tag further includes a passive RFID tag. 11) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 10, wherein the tag includes: 

 a) a base; b) a cover attached to the base to define an internal cavity containing tag components; and, c) bolts secured to opposing ends of the base to allow the tag to be coupled to a rail mounted on an unit loading device. 12) An article tracking system according to claim 11, wherein: a) the base includes a flat laminar metal plate bent to form a baselip extending at least part way around a perimeter of the base; and b) the cover includes a cover body that when coupled to the base defines a cavity that contains tag components, the cover body including a tongue extending laterally around at least part of a perimeter of the cover body that engages with the base lip to secure the cover to the base. 13) An article tracking system according to claim 12, wherein apertures extend perpendicularly through the base lip and tongue at opposing ends of the plate to define bolt openings adapted to receive the bolts. 14) An article tracking system according to claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the cover is at least one of: a) shaped to deflect impacts; and, b) made from at least one of: i) a plastic; and, ii) a Polycarbonate/Polybutyleneterephthalate mix. 15) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 12 to 14, wherein the tag includes a circuit board supporting electronic components and a battery housing, the battery housing including one or more recesses for receiving batteries and a ridged lip extending laterally outwardly from a lower edge of the battery housing body that sits in a recess in an underside face of the cover to thereby provide sealing engagement. 16) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 15, wherein the reader processing device causes the at least one reader transceiver to transmit at least one of: a) a broadcast message to cause the tag to enter an active operating mode; b) a sleep message to cause the tag to enter a sleep operating mode; and, c) a power message to cause the tag to enter a power off operating mode. 

 17) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 16, wherein the at least one reader transceiver transmits the broadcast message to any tags within an operating range of the reader, a tag processing device being responsive to the broadcast message to transmit the tag message. 18) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 17, wherein the at least one reader transceiver : a) receives a tag message; b) determines an identity of the tag from the tag message; and, c) provides a reader message to the reader processing device, the reader message being indicative of the identity of the tag, the reader processing device being responsive to the reader message to cause the at least one reader transceiver to generate at least one of a sleep message and a power message in accordance with the identity of the tag. 19) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 18, wherein the reader includes a plurality of reader transceivers that transmit or receive messages in parallel. 20) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 19, wherein the reader processing device generates commands to cause the at least one reader transceiver to generate messages. 21) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 20, wherein the reader processing device provides location data to the at least one processing system via a cellular communications network. 22) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 21, wherein the location data includes an indication of: a) a reader identifier indicative of an identity of the reader; and, b) a tag identifier indicative of an identify of the tag, and wherein the processing system: i) determines a reader location using the reader identifier; and, ii) determines an article identifier indicative of an identity of the article using the tag identifier and association data, the association data being indicative of an association between the article identifier and the tag identifier. 23) An article tracking system according to claim 22, wherein the at least one processing system: 

 a) receives an indication of an article identifier and a tag identifier during a registration process; and, b) creates the association data using the received indication. 24) An article tracking system according to claim 23, wherein at least one of the article identifier and tag identifier are determined by a scanner that scans machine readable coded data provided on at least one of the article and the tag. 25) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 22 to 24, wherein the at least one processing system at least one of: a) stores tracking data including an indication of the article location; and, b) generates a representation including an indication of a location of the article. 26) An article tracking system according to claim 25, wherein the representation includes at least one of: a) a map showing a location of the article; and, b) one of a number of visual indicia, the visual indicia being selected at least partially in accordance with the location. 27) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 1 to 26, wherein the tag readers are provided at transit locations within a transport network. 28) An article tracking system including at least one tracking tag attached to an article in use, the at least one tracking tag including: a) a power supply; b) a tag transceiver that transmits or receives messages; and c) a tag processing device that: i) in a passive operating mode, receives messages from a tag reader; and, ii) in accordance with a received message, at least one of: (1) changes a tag operating mode; and, (2) generates a tag message for transmission to the reader, the tag message being at least partially indicative of an identity of the tag and being used in determining a location of the article. 29) An article tracking system according to claim 28, wherein in the passive operating mode the tag processing device does not transmit messages. 

 30) An article tracking system according to claim 28 or claim 29, wherein in response to a broadcast message received from the tag reader, the tag processing device: a) enters an active operating mode; and, b) transmits the tag message. 31) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 2306, wherein the tag processing device: a) receives a sleep message from the reader; and, b) enters a sleep operating mode for a defined time period in response to receiving the sleep message, wherein in the sleep operating mode the tag processing device at least one of: i) does not respond to messages; and, ii) does not receive messages. 32) An article tracking system according to claim 31, wherein the sleep message is generated by the reader in response to receipt of the tag message. 33) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 32, wherein in response to a power message, the tag processing device enters a power off operating mode by deactivating the power supply. 34) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 33, wherein the tag message includes: a) a tag identifier indicative of the tag identity; and, b) at least one of: i) a power supply indicator indicative of a power supply status; and, ii) a temperature indicator indicative of the maximum and minimum temperature to which the tag has been exposed between interactions with a reader. 35) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 34, wherein the tag transceiver is a Bluetooth transceiver. 36) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 35, wherein the tag is labelled with information including at least one of: a) machine readable coded data indicative of a tag identifier; b) coded data indicative of the identity of the tag; and, c) visible markings indicative of an identity of the tag. 

 37) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 36, wherein the tag further includes a passive RFID tag. 38) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 28 to 37, wherein the tag includes: a) a base; b) a cover attached to the base to define an internal cavity containing tag components; and, c) bolts secured to opposing ends of the base to allow the tag to be coupled to a rail mounted on an unit loading device. 39) An article tracking system according to claim 38, wherein: a) the base includes a flat laminar metal plate bent to form a baselip extending at least part way around a perimeter of the base; and b) the cover includes a cover body that when coupled to the base defines a cavity that contains tag components, the cover body including a tongue extending laterally around at least part of a perimeter of the cover body that engages with the base lip to secure the cover to the base. 40) An article tracking system according to claim 39, wherein apertures extend perpendicularly through the base lip and tongue at opposing ends of the plate to define bolt openings adapted to receive the bolts. 41) An article tracking system according to claim 39 or claim 40, wherein the cover is at least one of: a) shaped to deflect impacts; and, b) made from at least one of: i) a plastic; and, ii) a Polycarbonate/Polybutyleneterephthalate mix. 42) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 39 to 41, wherein the tag includes a circuit board supporting electronic components and a battery housing, the battery housing including one or more recesses for receiving batteries and a ridged lip extending laterally outwardly from a lower edge of the battery housing body that sits in a recess in an underside face of the cover to thereby provide sealing engagement. 

 43) 28An article tracking system including at least one tracking tag attached to an article in use, and a reader including: a) at least one reader transceiver that transmits or receives messages; and, b) a reader processing device, wherein the reader processing device and the at least one reader transceiver communicate to: i) generate a broadcast message for transmission to a tag; and, ii) receive a tag message from the tag, the tag message being at least partially indicative of an identity of the tag and being used in determining a location of the article. 44) An article tracking system according to claim 43, wherein the reader processing device causes the at least one reader transceiver to transmit at least one of: a) a broadcast message to cause the tag to enter an active operating mode; b) a sleep message to cause the tag to enter a sleep operating mode; and, c) a power message to cause the tag to enter a power off operating mode. 45) An article tracking system according to claim 44, wherein the at least one reader transceiver transmits the broadcast message to any tags within an operating range of the reader, a tag processing device being responsive to the broadcast message to transmit the tag message. 46) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 43 to 45, wherein the at least one reader transceiver: a) receives the tag message; b) determines an identity of the tag from the tag message; and, c) provides a reader message to the reader processing device, the reader messages being indicative of the identity of the tag, the reader processing device being responsive to the reader message to cause the at least one reader transceiver to generate at least one of a sleep message and a power message in accordance with the identity of the tag. 47) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 43 to 46, wherein the reader includes a plurality of reader transceivers that transmit or receive messages in parallel. 48) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 43 to 47, wherein the reader processing device provides location data to a processing system, the location data being at least partially indicative of a location of the tag. 

 49) An article tracking system according to claim 48, wherein the location data includes an indication of: a) a reader identifier indicative of an identity of the reader; and, b) a tag identifier indicative of an identify of the tag. 50) An article tracking system according to claim 48 or claim 49, wherein the reader processing device provides location data to the processing system via a cellular communications network. 51) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 43 to 50, wherein the reader processing device generates commands to cause the at least one reader transceiver to generate messages. 52) An article tracking system including: a) a plurality of tracking tags, each tag being attached to an article in use; b) a number of readers that: i) communicate with tracking tags to determine an identity of each tag; and, ii) generate location data at least partially indicative of a location of each tag; c) at least one processing system in communication with the number of readers to: i) determine the location data; and, ii) track the location of articles using the location data. 53) An article tracking system according to claim 52, wherein the location data includes an indication of: a) a reader identifier indicative of an identity of the reader; and, b) a tag identifier indicative of an identify of the tag, and wherein the processing system: i) determines a reader location using the reader identifier; and, ii) determines an article identifier indicative of an identity of the article using the tag identifier and association data, the association data being indicative of an association between the article identifier and the tag identifier. 54) An article tracking system according to claim 53, wherein the processing system: a) receives an indication of an article identifier and a tag identifier during a registration process; and, b) creates the association data using the received indication. 

 55) An article tracking system according to claim 54, wherein at least one of the article identifier and tag identifier are determined by a scanner that scans machine readable coded data provided on at least one of the article and the tag. 56) An article tracking system according to claim 54 or claim 55, wherein the processing system at least one of: a) stores tracking data including an indication of the article location; and, b) generates a representation including an indication of a location of the article. 57) An article tracking system according to claim 56, wherein the representation includes at least one of: a) a map showing a location of the article; and, b) one of a number of visual indicia, the visual indicia being selected at least partially in accordance with the location. 58) An article tracking system according to any one of the claims 52 to 57, wherein the readers are provided at transit locations within a transport network. 59) An article tracking method using at least one tracking tag attached to an article in use, the method including in a tag processing device of the tag: a) in a passive operating mode, receiving messages from a tag reader; and, b) in accordance with a received message, at least one of: i) changing a tag operating mode; and, ii) generating a tag message for transmission to the reader, the tag message being at least partially indicative of an identity of the tag and being used in determining a location of the article. 60) A method of tracking an article using at least one tracking tag attached to an article in use, the method including, in a reader processing device of a tag reader: i) generating a broadcast message for transmission to a tag; and, ii) receiving a tag message from the tag, the tag message being at least partially indicative of an identity of the tag and being used in determining a location of the article. 61) A method of tracking an article using an article tracking system including: a) a plurality of tracking tags, each tag being attached to an article in use; b) a number of tag readers; and, 

 c) at least one processing system in communication with the number of readers, the method including: i) in the tag reader: (1) communicating with tracking tags to determine an identity of each tag; and, (2) generating location data at least partially indicative of a location of each tag; ii) in the processing system: (1) determining the location data; and, (2) using the location data to track the location of articles.
